steps.title: Spot the sentence boundary problem
steps.content: The sentence shows “she ______ the 1960 Summer Olympics in Rome, she won both the 100- and 200-meter dashes…” Two independent clauses are separated by a comma: what she accomplished during the Olympics, and what medals she won.

steps.title: Apply the sentence boundary rule
steps.content: Two independent clauses cannot be separated by a comma alone—use a period instead. Option D places a period after “fast,” and capitalizes “During” to begin a new sentence: “During the 1960 Summer Olympics in Rome, she won both the 100- and 200-meter dashes…” This properly separates the two ideas. Options A, B, and C maintain the comma between the two clauses, creating a comma splice. That matches choice D — D is correct.
